content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

January 8, 2004

Glenn Sabine, City Attorney
City of La Mesa
Post Office Box 937
La Mesa, CA 91944-0937

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-03-245

Dear Mr. Sabine:

This correspondence is written notification that the Fair Political Practices Commission ("FPPC") has withdrawn your October 22, 2003, request on behalf of Vice Mayor Ernest Ewin for written advice under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

I have left voicemail messages on December 9, 2003 and December 16, 2003, and sent you a letter on December 23, 2003, requesting the additional information necessary to answer your request for advice, and have not received a response from you. Since we do not have the information to respond to your request, it has been withdrawn. (Regulation 18329 (c)(4)(F), copy enclosed.) You may submit another request for advice once you have gathered the necessary facts. If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Galena West
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure
GW:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\03-245

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: Vice Mayor Ernest Ewin requested written advice from the FPPC regarding an unspecified matter under the Political Reform Act, but the requestor did not provide necessary additional information after multiple follow-up attempts by the FPPC.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC has withdrawn the request for advice because the City Attorney failed to provide essential information needed to respond, despite voicemail messages on December 9 and 16, 2003, and a letter dated December 23, 2003. The request may be resubmitted once the necessary facts are gathered.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter, not a standard advice letter. No substantive question or conclusion sections are present. The document clearly indicates withdrawal under Regulation 18329(c)(a)(b). Document contains voicemail and letter dates showing follow-up attempts by FPPC.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Vice Mayor Ernest Ewin requested written advice from the FPPC regarding an unspecified matter under the Political Reform Act, but the requestor did not provide necessary additional information after multiple follow-up attempts by the FPPC.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC has withdrawn the request for advice because the City Attorney failed to provide essential information needed to respond, despite voicemail messages on December 9 and 16, 2003, and a letter dated December 23, 2003. The request may be resubmitted once the necessary facts are gathered.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew Vice Mayor Ernest Ewin's October 22, 2003 request for written advice due to lack of response to requests for additional information. The requestor may resubmit once the necessary facts are provided.
